items.t: — Canada's first diamond mine enters receivership; production stops mid-August, ~340 jobs wind down, C$326M cleanup fund shielded
— Endiama tables an offer for Anglo American's full 85%, valued at $4.9B after write-downs; Botswana holds the right to match
— the CPI pop fades on day five of US strikes on Iranian sites; silver slips to 57.07, platinum eases to 1,634.90
— wholesale prices −13% y/y but one-carat rounds rise 1%, the category's only gainer, per Edahn Golan's price list
— the Chronomat slims to 13.77mm with a one-piece bezel and new 78-hour B31 calibre from £5,250; Watches & Wonders debut set for 2027
— the Kyiv-founded jeweler opens its first US boutique at 112 Wooster Street, SoHo, in its tenth-anniversary year
— June sales reach $768.6B, below forecast, as tax-refund tailwinds fade and tariff caution sets in
— Nolan's epic reaches the counter: Roseate's pearl medallions from $275 land at Nordstrom; intaglio revival builds
— the no-buyer's-premium monthly closes its sixth edition in New York: eleven white-diamond lots, hammer price is the price
